title: cienfuegosia affinis var. humilis
intro: Cienfuegosia affinis var. humilis, also known as the dwarf firebush, is a perennial shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a species of the Cienfuegosia genus, characterized by its small size and vibrant red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and poultices, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used as a natural remedy for various ailments.
